arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line objdump readelf 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in the backtrace. The tombstone shows frames from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with a Build ID of `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. 

To symbolicate these frames, I would need the debug symbols for that specific build of the Mono runtime. This requires:

1. The matching debug symbols (`.so` with debug info, or a `.dbg` file) for the exact build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so` identified by BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`


2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or `ndk-stack` to resolve the addresses

The Build ID uniquely identifies this specific Mono runtime build. These debug symbols are typically available from the .NET MAUI SDK or NuGet packages, or from Microsoft's symbol server. Since I have `curl` available, I could attempt to fetch the symbols directly from Microsoft's symbol server using the standard URL pattern.

For ELF files with build IDs, the symbol server uses a specific format. I'm considering whether to try the Microsoft symbol server directly or use the debuginfod protocol approach...

Actually, I should be straightforward here—I don't have access to the actual debug symbols for this specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, and I'm missing the Android NDK tools like `ndk-stack` and `addr2line` for aarch64 that would be needed to properly symbolicate the stack trace.

Let me try downloading the symbols from Microsoft's symbol server using curl and see what symbolication tools are available on this system.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try the ELF debug info format
curl -sSL -o /tmp/mono_dbg.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" -w "\nHTTP: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1 | tail -5

# Also try the _.debug variant
curl -sSL -o /tmp/mono_dbg2.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg" -w "\nHTTP: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1 | tail -5
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
We have `addr2line` but not `llvm-symbolizer`. We need the debug symbols for the Mono runtime. Let me try to download them from Microsoft's symbol server.

The Build ID is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. Let me try downloading the symbols from Microsoft's symbol server.

For ELF files, the Microsoft symbol server URL pattern is:
`https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/<filename>`

Let me try this.

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try the ELF debug info format
curl -sSL -o /tmp/mono_dbg.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" -w "\nHTTP: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1 | tail -5

# Also try the _.debug variant
curl -sSL -o /tmp/mono_dbg2.so "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg" -w "\nHTTP: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1 | tail -5
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server
